title: pastinaca palmata
intro: Pastinaca palmata, also known as the wild carrot, is a biennial herb native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, valued for its medicinal properties, including antispasmodic, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory issues, and menstrual cramps, offering benefits such as relieving nausea, reducing fever, and easing pain. In herbal preparations, the root and leaves of the wild carrot are typically used, often in the form of teas, tinctures, or infusions.
